caseStudy.title: Synthetic Data Generation Pipeline
caseStudy.description: Augmenting scarce training data with photorealistic synthetics.
caseStudy.overview: An autonomous robotics company was bottlenecked by the cost and time required to collect and label real-world training data for its navigation models. A synthetic data pipeline was developed to generate photorealistic training scenes at scale.
caseStudy.problem: Collecting and labelling 10,000 real-world images for a new environment type took 6 weeks and cost $120,000. Rare edge cases — such as unusual lighting conditions or obstacle configurations — were nearly impossible to capture in sufficient quantities from real data collection.
caseStudy.solution: A synthetic data generation pipeline was built using a real-time 3D renderer, procedurally generating scenes with randomised lighting, textures, object placements, and camera angles. Domain randomisation ensured models trained on synthetics transferred effectively to real environments. Automatic pixel-perfect labelling eliminated manual annotation.

stats.label: Cost reduction vs real data collection

results.summary: The synthetic pipeline has fundamentally changed the economics of training data at the company. New environment types can now be supported in days rather than months, accelerating the product roadmap.
